title: Asset collection silently swallows copy errors with 2>/dev/null || true
reasoning: Lines 198-199 use `cp ... 2>/dev/null || true` to copy bundle artifacts. While the glob may legitimately match nothing if a directory is empty, this pattern also hides real errors such as disk-full, permission denied, or I/O failures. The downstream manifest completeness check (line 218) partially mitigates missing bundles, but a partial copy (e.g., truncated file due to disk pressure) would pass the count check while shipping a corrupt asset.
reproduction: Fill the runner disk nearly full so that `cp` fails mid-write; the error is suppressed and a truncated tarball ends up in release-assets.
recommendation: Remove `2>/dev/null` and handle the no-match case explicitly: use `find` or check glob expansion (`shopt -s nullglob`) so that a missing file is not an error, but a real copy failure still triggers `set -e`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests cover the workflow. The error suppression is only observable under I/O failure conditions in CI.
minimumFixScope: Replace `2>/dev/null || true` with `shopt -s nullglob` before the loop and remove the error suppression so real cp failures propagate.
